agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences for users. Bird provided two submissions; v2 supersedes v1 after legal review tightened the rules on transactional emails. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird's v2. The scope is narrow: a single PATCH endpoint for toggling marketing emails, with a hard block on disabling transactional emails.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected; the system must never persist a state where transactional emails are disabled for any user"},{"rule":"Marketing emails are optional and user-controllable","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false, and the persisted value reflects their choice"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences","implementation_note":"Accept a JSON body with optional boolean fields. Only marketing_emails_enabled is mutable. If transactional_emails_enabled is present and set to false, reject the entire request before persisting anything."},{"decision":"Return 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED when transactional opt-out is attempted","implementation_note":"Do not return 400 (bad request). Bird's AC3 explicitly specifies 403 and the error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. This signals a policy prohibition, not a malformed request."},{"decision":"Store marketing preference as a boolean field marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default value for new users should be true (opted in). Confirm with Bird if a different default is needed, but assume true unless told otherwise."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"Response is 200, and marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back in to marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"Response is 200, and marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"Response is 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. No preferences are changed."},{"criterion":"Transactional field set to true is a no-op (not an error)","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"Response is 200. This is a benign assertion of the current required state and should not be rejected."}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-sent emails required for account security and operations (password resets, security alerts). Cannot be disabled per legal mandate."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and informational emails. Fully user-controllable opt-in/opt-out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while also stating a domain rule that transactional emails cannot be fully disabled. Bird v2 removed AC2 entirely and hardened the domain rule after legal review.","resolution":"v2 supersedes v1. There is no transactional opt-out capability. MJ's architecture aligns with v2 — the API rejects any attempt to disable transactional emails. No conflict remains."}],"open_questions":["What is the default value of marketing_emails_enabled for newly created users? Assumed true (opted in) — confirm with Bird if legal requires explicit opt-in (default false).","Should the endpoint be idempotent for unchanged values (e.g., setting marketing_emails_enabled to its current value)? Assumed yes — return 200 with current state.","Authorization scope: can a user only update their own preferences, or can admins update on behalf of others? Not specified — implement for self-service only and flag if admin access is needed."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and legally grounded after Bird v2","MJ's architecture fully aligns with Bird v2 — no conflicts","AC3 (403 for transactional opt-out) is explicitly specified by both Bird and MJ"],"low_confidence_areas":["Default value for marketing_emails_enabled on new users not explicitly stated","Authorization model (self-only vs admin) not addressed by either agent","Behavior when transactional_emails_enabled is sent as true (benign no-op) — inferred, not specified"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 fully supersedes v1; v1 acceptance criteria that were removed are intentionally excluded","marketing_emails_enabled defaults to true for new users","Setting transactional_emails_enabled to true is accepted as a harmless no-op rather than rejected"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the inputs from Bird and MJ for the "notification preferences" feature.

Bird has two submissions - v1 and v2. v2 is the later one and supersedes v1. Key changes: AC2 (transactional email opt-out) was removed in v2, and AC3 was added to enforce the prohibition via API.

MJ's submission aligns with Bird's v2 - the API validates that transactional_emails_enabled cannot be set to false and returns 403.

Classification: `none` - all outputs are consistent. Bird v2 and MJ agree. 

Now I'll prepare the handoff brief.

content.text: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences for users. Bird provided two submissions; v2 supersedes v1 after legal review tightened the rules on transactional emails. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird's v2. The scope is narrow: a single PATCH endpoint for toggling marketing emails, with a hard block on disabling transactional emails.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected; the system must never persist a state where transactional emails are disabled for any user"},{"rule":"Marketing emails are optional and user-controllable","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false, and the persisted value reflects their choice"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences","implementation_note":"Accept a JSON body with optional boolean fields. Only marketing_emails_enabled is mutable. If transactional_emails_enabled is present and set to false, reject the entire request before persisting anything."},{"decision":"Return 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED when transactional opt-out is attempted","implementation_note":"Do not return 400 (bad request). Bird's AC3 explicitly specifies 403 and the error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. This signals a policy prohibition, not a malformed request."},{"decision":"Store marketing preference as a boolean field marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default value for new users should be true (opted in). Confirm with Bird if a different default is needed, but assume true unless told otherwise."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"Response is 200, and marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back in to marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"Response is 200, and marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"Response is 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. No preferences are changed."},{"criterion":"Transactional field set to true is a no-op (not an error)","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"Response is 200. This is a benign assertion of the current required state and should not be rejected."}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-sent emails required for account security and operations (password resets, security alerts). Cannot be disabled per legal mandate."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and informational emails. Fully user-controllable opt-in/opt-out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while also stating a domain rule that transactional emails cannot be fully disabled. Bird v2 removed AC2 entirely and hardened the domain rule after legal review.","resolution":"v2 supersedes v1. There is no transactional opt-out capability. MJ's architecture aligns with v2 — the API rejects any attempt to disable transactional emails. No conflict remains."}],"open_questions":["What is the default value of marketing_emails_enabled for newly created users? Assumed true (opted in) — confirm with Bird if legal requires explicit opt-in (default false).","Should the endpoint be idempotent for unchanged values (e.g., setting marketing_emails_enabled to its current value)? Assumed yes — return 200 with current state.","Authorization scope: can a user only update their own preferences, or can admins update on behalf of others? Not specified — implement for self-service only and flag if admin access is needed."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and legally grounded after Bird v2","MJ's architecture fully aligns with Bird v2 — no conflicts","AC3 (403 for transactional opt-out) is explicitly specified by both Bird and MJ"],"low_confidence_areas":["Default value for marketing_emails_enabled on new users not explicitly stated","Authorization model (self-only vs admin) not addressed by either agent","Behavior when transactional_emails_enabled is sent as true (benign no-op) — inferred, not specified"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 fully supersedes v1; v1 acceptance criteria that were removed are intentionally excluded","marketing_emails_enabled defaults to true for new users","Setting transactional_emails_enabled to true is accepted as a harmless no-op rather than rejected"]}}
